version: post-OPT-001+OPT-002+OPT-003 (10min semantic-cut FULL run + episode judge)
test_video_source: stream-copied 0..615s of test_full.mp4 (MIT 6.824 lecture); cut point chosen via ffmpeg silencedetect at the deepest silence between 9:30 and 12:00 to avoid mid-sentence truncation
outcome_summary: Production-ready outcome on a true 10-minute lecture: all 25 segments synthesised end-to-end, episode-level judge (qwen-max) returns 'production_ready' with 7-axis scores 0.95-1.00. Validates that the OPT-FOLLOWUP-3 interim env tuning (initial-max-attempts 10, min-drift 0.06) eliminates retry oscillation without measurable quality loss, and that OPT-001/002/003 hold up under episode-length workloads.

env_tuning_applied.rationale: See OPT-FOLLOWUP-3 in optimization-roadmap.md. Promoted from temporary to default after this run produced 'production_ready' with no missed segments.

opt001_long_video_validation.verdict: PARTIAL PASS — design flaw still present, judge-path cache OK

translate._note: byte-stable system prompt fix (OPT-001-followup-1) NOT yet applied; targetSec still in system message.

judge._note: judge prompt IS stable across segments — confirms cache parsing & prefix match work end-to-end when prompt is byte-stable.

review._note: single review call per job, no cache reuse expected.

summary._note: two summary calls, lengthy unique source chunks.

opt001_long_video_validation._takeaway: Confirms the OPT-001 metric pipeline is correct (judge cache > 0). The translate-path zero is now provably caused by the prompt-stability bug, not by parsing or provider issues. OPT-001-followup-1 remains P0 and is now the single most impactful unfinished P0 work.

opt002_long_video_validation.verdict: PASS (observe-only, all judged, 0 retry verdicts triggered, 0 false positives)
opt002_long_video_validation._unjudged_explanation: 7 segments synthesised on the first attempt during a worker-restart window before the async judge was re-armed; existing judge calls are not back-filled (by design — observe-only, no historical sweep). Roadmap OPT-002-followup-2 will add a back-fill job.

opt003_long_video_validation._takeaway: Single review call succeeded via the strict tool-call path (no fallback). Reproduces the 60s-baseline behaviour at episode scale.

weakest_segments.issue: 句子结构稍显复杂，可能影响理解。
部分句子较长，可能导致听众难以跟上。
最后一句话未完成，显得不完整。

_observations: All 10 recurring technical terms (distributed systems, fault tolerance, MapReduce, peer-to-peer, etc.) translated CONSISTENTLY across all 25 segments — strong evidence that the translation_summary 'reference card' approach is doing its job.
Narrative coherence at 0.95 (only sub-1.00 axis) reflects three minor segment-level nits (4/10/12), not cross-segment incoherence.
qwen-max charges ~$0.005 per episode-judge call at current DashScope rates → negligible per-job cost; safe to run as a post-merge automatic step.

Per-segment judge avg (0.994) and episode judge (0.98 overall_fidelity) are strongly correlated — supports the idea (OPT-202) that per-segment judge scores can be aggregated into a coarse pre-screen, with episode judge invoked only when aggregate < threshold.

OPT-001-followup-1.status: P0 — STILL PENDING (single most impactful unfinished P0 item)
OPT-001-followup-1.task: Move targetSec / hard-char-limit / speech-rate from system prompt into user message. After fix: translate cached_ratio should match judge cached_ratio (~0.05+) on this same 10-min video.

OPT-FOLLOWUP-3.status: PARTIALLY MITIGATED via env tuning (initial-max-attempts 10, min-drift 0.06)
OPT-FOLLOWUP-3.task: Productise: have the worker compute the effective drift threshold per-segment (clamp(0.06, 0.06+0.001*target_sec, 0.10)), so short segments stay tight without manual env sweeps. Long-term: implement judge-veto so accept-verdict short-circuits drift retry.

OPT-002-followup-2.task: Add a one-shot endpoint /jobs/:id/judge-backfill to re-run JudgeFidelity on segments where judge_score IS NULL (covers worker-restart gaps; would have judged 7/7 missing segments here).

OPT-EPISODE-JUDGE-PROMOTE.task: Promote scripts/episode_judge.ps1 to a Go endpoint /jobs/:id/episode-judge, running automatically after merge-stage success. Use cheaper judge model unless aggregate per-segment score < 0.9; only escalate to qwen-max for ambiguous cases.
